title: salix × mariana
intro: Salix × mariana, also known as the gray or river cottonwood, is a hybrid of the white willow (Salix alba) and the goat willow (Salix caprea), and is native to northern Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the salicylic acid it contains, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × mariana include relieving pain, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Salix × mariana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in tinctures and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
